title: Emergency Aid for Displaced Al-Hajri Family — Aden, Yemen
story: My family of eight — including my elderly mother and four children under twelve — fled Hudaydah in late 2025 after renewed shelling destroyed our home and neighborhood market. We are now sheltering in a rented room in Aden with no income, depleted savings, and no access to regular employment. We urgently need funds for food, medicines for my mother's heart condition, and three months of rent while we seek stable resettlement. Several community elders and religious leaders in Aden who know our family personally have written to confirm our circumstances and vouch for the legitimacy of this appeal.
vouching_narrative: Sheikh Abdulrahman Bin Saeed Al-Wazir, senior imam of Masjid Al-Noor in Crater district, Aden, provided a signed attestation confirming he has known the Al-Hajri family for over a decade and personally witnessed their displacement from Hudaydah. Mr. Hassan Mohammed Al-Zubayri, a neighborhood council representative in Khor Maksar, Aden, and Mrs. Fatima Qasim Al-Baydhani, coordinator for the Aden Women's Relief Network, co-signed a community reference letter corroborating the family's displacement, the number of dependents, and the absence of any formal income. Funds will be received by Tariq Al-Hajri and disbursed under oversight of Sheikh Al-Wazir's mosque committee, which tracks all distributions to displaced families in the district.
